title: Podman machine start failed (unable to connect to "gvproxy" socket)
body:
### Issue Description

Failed to start podman machine:
```
Error: unable to connect to "gvproxy" socket at "/var/folders/s7/tb66rr3940j0v9tsg84dcz0w0000gn/T/podman/tqt-gvproxy.sock"
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman machine init (root or rootless, doesn't matter)
2. podman machine start

machine remove and re-init doesn't help


### Describe the results you received

gvproxy.log -> looks strange `/private/var/run/docker.sock": no such file or directory`
```
time="2025-01-23T13:21:21+03:00" level=info msg="gvproxy version v0.8.2"
time="2025-01-23T13:21:21+03:00" level=info msg="reloading dns nameservers to [192.168.0.1:53 192.168.0.1:53]"
time="2025-01-23T13:21:21+03:00" level=error msg="gvproxy exiting: cannot add network services: adding watcher failed: \"/private/var/run/docker.sock\": no such file or directory"
```

podman machine debug logs
```
❯ podman --log-level=debug machine start
time="2025-01-23T13:21:21+03:00" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"

```

### Describe the results you expected

podman machine to be started

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: darwin/amd64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Note we do recommend using our own macos installer over brew in general. Out installer should have an older gpvroxy version that does not have this issue but you could also manually replace the gvproxy version

I have the same issue and also on MacOS 14.7.2 (23H311) with homwbrew installer being used since updating to podman 5.3.2.

I switched to the officail installer using the podman-desktop variant, which installs podman 5.3.1 (!) - that version was working well with homebrew too ;-)
But this might be a workaround until the issue is getting fixed.

I created a discussion on homebrew regading this:

https://github.com/orgs/Homebrew/discussions/5901
